content.claim: UN Special Rapporteur Farida Shaheed (2025 country visit) assessed that the growing number of children with SEN has not been matched by adequate teacher training or specialist support, with larger classes and documented shortages of specialists such as doctors and sign language interpreters presenting barriers to accommodation.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Rapporteur assessment is qualitative rather than a quantified capacity audit
No independent statistical verification of claimed specialist shortages provided
Assessment reflects external human-rights standard rather than Hungarian baseline data
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
